DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-24-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS EDWARD D. REISKIN, IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS THE DIRECTOR OF TRANSPORTATION FOR THE SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY and SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Appearances: Terence J. Howzell of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.3960) for defendants and moving parties; Brent Robinson of the Liberation Law Group (415.695.1000) for plaintiffs and responding parties.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Overruled. Plaintiff states a cause of action for breach of contract. The CBA agreement is enforceable under Freeman v. State Farm Mut. Auto Ins. Co. (1975) 14 Cal.3d 473, 480 and Retired Employees Assn. of Orange County Inc. v. County of Orange (2011) 52 Cal.4th 1171, 1182. The Court cannot determine that the claim is subject to the CBA's grievance process. Neither party asks the Court to take judicial notice of the CBA and Plantiff's interpretation of the CBA at 4:18-20 of the Complaint is entitled to deference. See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ("While plaintiffs' interpretation of the contract may prove invalid, it [is] improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading. 'In ruling on a demurrer, the likelihood that the pleading will be able to prove his allegations is not the question.'"). The demurrer to the PAGA cause of action is overruled. The Court cannot determine whether there is a conflict between the CBA and Labor Code because a complete copy of the CBA was not provided to the Court. The demurrer to the petition for writ of mandate cause of action is overruled. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR #6185 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-02-2013 Continued To Oct-24-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-16-2013 Continued To Oct-17-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs EDWARD D. REISKIN, IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS THE DIRECTOR OF TRANSPORTATION FOR THE SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY, and SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued on the Court's own motion to October 17, 2013 at 9:30 am. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
